summary: The capsule correctly identifies the trigger (upstream timeouts on /slow), the root cause (connection refused from app-svc at 14:08), and the 'no live upstreams' escalation, but misses the [crit]-severity 'no servers are available' entries and the worker-process restart at 14:10 that marks service recovery.
improvements: Include at least one [crit]-level 'no servers are available' line (e.g. line 107) — it is a severity escalation beyond [error] and marks the backend as fully exhausted, a materially different state
Add the 'worker process 5 started' notice at 14:10:37 (line 121) as a recovery context entry so engineers can determine the duration of the outage without scrolling the full log
